content: We can frame Self-Refine's iterative self-improvement loop as a single-perspective base case that can be extended by incorporating multiple inconsistent perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hybrid framework that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra training or supervised data while boosting refinement quality through multi-perspective inconsistency detection. This hybrid framework would not only refine output based on generic self-feedback but also actively surface hidden flaws that a single self-feedback loop would miss, outperforming vanilla Self-Refine on complex tasks like code debugging and open-ended reasoning.
